
**Branch**: `004-dashboard-refactor` | **Date**: 2026-04-29 | **Spec**: [specs/004-dashboard-refactor/spec.md](specs/004-dashboard-refactor/spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-dashboard-refactor/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by `/speckit.plan` and strictly follows the clarified spec and Constitution v1.3.0 (Principle XI).

## Summary

Refactor the dashboard page to deliver a premium, visually rich, and highly usable experience, strictly adhering to the new Dashboard UX & Data Visualization Standards (Principle XI). The dashboard will present all Sahim services/features in a clear priority order, using a 12-column responsive grid, premium dark design, and appropriate Chart.js visualizations. All navigation, tier gating, and accessibility requirements are enforced. The dashboard acts as a navigation hub, not a feature surface.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (frontend), Python 3.11 (backend)
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, react-hook-form + zod, next-intl, Firebase Auth/Firestore, Chart.js (react-chartjs-2), FastAPI, Pydantic v2, httpx, NumPy, statsmodels
**Storage**: Firestore (user/watchlist/profile), backend REST APIs
**Testing**: Jest (frontend), pytest (backend)
**Target Platform**: Web (desktop/mobile/tablet), PWA-capable
**Project Type**: SaaS web application (frontend/backend)
**Performance Goals**: Dashboard loads all 6 zones (with live data) in <3s on broadband; skeletons appear <100ms; backend handles 100 concurrent users
**Constraints**: No hardcoded secrets; all user-facing text must be bilingual; all charts use Chart.js via react-chartjs-2; no inline service UIs; strict WCAG 2.1 AA compliance; tier gating enforced server-side
**Scale/Scope**: 1 dashboard page, 6 layout zones, 9 service cards, 4+ chart types, 2+ locales, 3+ user tiers

## Constitution Check

**GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.**

- I (Demo-Day First): Feature is scoped for completion before May 1st deadline
- III (Arabic-First): All labels, tooltips, and UI text must be available in Arabic (ar.json)
- IV (Halal Integrity): Halal screening outputs display mandatory disclaimer
- V (Regulatory Compliance): All risk/ARIMA outputs display investment-advice disclaimer
- VII (Security): No hardcoded secrets; all user input validated at API boundary
- VIII (SaaS Architecture): Dashboard requires authentication; tier gating enforced server-side
- IX (Component & Accessibility): Tailwind-only, keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, WCAG AA contrast
- X (Competitive Differentiation): Dashboard prioritizes Arabic-first, halal-native, beginner-safe mission; methodology transparency present
- XI (Dashboard Design): All dashboard zones, chart types, card order, and design language comply with Principle XI

**No violations detected. All gates pass.**

```text
backend/

frontend/
├── package.json
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── [locale]/
│   │   │   └── dashboard/page.tsx   # Dashboard page (main entrypoint)
│   │   └── globals.css
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── dashboard/ServiceCardGrid.tsx
│   │   ├── TickerStrip.tsx
│   │   ├── RiskPanel.tsx
│   │   ├── RiskWizard.tsx
│   │   ├── SectorPanel.tsx
│   │   ├── ArimaChart.tsx
│   │   ├── PurificationCalculator.tsx
│   │   ├── ComplianceNotificationBanner.tsx
│   │   ├── ComplianceAlertToggle.tsx
│   │   ├── ParticleBackground.tsx
│   │   ├── DataFreshnessLabel.tsx
│   │   ├── TrafficLightBadge.tsx
│   │   ├── LanguageSwitcher.tsx
│   │   ├── NavBar.tsx
│   │   ├── NewsPanel.tsx
│   │   └── ui/  # Shared primitives (Button, Card, Badge, Modal, etc.)
│   ├── hooks/
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── services.ts
│   │   ├── types.ts
│   │   └── api.ts
│   ├── messages/
│   │   ├── ar.json
│   │   └── en.json
│   └── providers/
└── public/
  └── images/
```

**Structure Decision**: The project is a full-stack SaaS web app with a Next.js frontend and FastAPI backend. The dashboard refactor will update/extend `frontend/src/app/[locale]/dashboard/page.tsx`, related dashboard components, and supporting hooks/lib/types. Backend endpoints for ARIMA, sector, news, and risk data are already present; only minor adjustments may be needed for new data shapes or error handling.
